Why This Data — The Connection to the Project

The Accessibility Monitor's Audit Agent needs two things to work: (1) a complete reference of every WCAG 2.1 rule with its criterion ID, conformance level, and description — so it can map axe-core violations back to the exact WCAG requirement violated, and (2) a live feed of accessibility industry news so the Report Agent can include context like "this violation type increased 18% industry-wide in Q1 2025" in its digest emails. This pipeline collects both, plus the axe-core rule definitions that power the audit engine itself.

Part 1 · n8n Workflow
⭐ Error Handling + Source Integration
Working Data Collection Workflow — 8 Nodes

Workflow file: Singh_Kanishk_A3_Workflow.json · Run at: http://localhost:5678

Workflow Overview — Node by Node
NODE 1
Manual
Trigger
→
NODE 2
GitHub API
Axe-core Rules
→
NODE 3
HTTP Request
W3C WCAG JSON
→
NODE 4
RSS Feed
WebAIM Blog
+
NODE 4b
RSS Feed
A11y Project
→
NODE 5
Code Node
Merge + Clean
→
NODE 6
IF Node
Error Check
→
NODE 7
Write File
CSV Output
Node Configurations
▶
Node 1 · Trigger
Manual Trigger
Type: n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger — Kicks off the workflow on demand. Also has a secondary Schedule Trigger set to run every Sunday at 02:00 UTC so the WCAG data stays current with any W3C spec updates. The manual trigger is used for the initial collection run and testing.
GH
Node 2 · Source 1 — Axe-core Rules
HTTP Request → GitHub Contents API
URL: https://api.github.com/repos/dequelabs/axe-core/contents/lib/rules
Method: GET · Auth: None required (public repo, 60 req/hour unauthenticated)
Returns: JSON array of rule file metadata. Returned 105 items on last run (13:22:45, 23 May 2026 — completed in 1.508s). Extracts id, tags (wcag2a, wcag2aa, wcag21aa), description, and help text per rule. Uses axe-core version 4.10.
Error handling: onError: continueRegularOutput — if GitHub rate-limits or returns 403, the node logs "Source 1 unavailable" and passes an empty array to the merge node. The workflow continues with Sources 2 and 3.
W3
Node 3 · Source 2 — WCAG 2.1 Success Criteria
HTTP Request → W3C WCAG JSON-LD
URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/w3c/wcag/main/guidelines/wcag.json
Method: GET · Source: W3C's official WCAG repository on GitHub (raw file, no auth needed)
Returns: Structured JSON of all WCAG 2.1 success criteria. Returned 1 item (the full JSON object, expanded to 78 success criteria in Node 5). Completed in 138ms on last run.
Error handling: Same continueRegularOutput pattern. Fallback: a static WCAG criteria CSV is bundled in the repo as a last-resort data source if W3C GitHub is unreachable.
RSS
Node 4 · Source 3 — Accessibility Industry News
RSS Feed Reader — WebAIM Blog + A11y Project
Feed 1: https://webaim.org/blog/feed/ (WebAIM — leading accessibility research org)
Feed 2: https://www.a11yproject.com/feed/feed.xml (A11y Project — practitioner community)
Node type: n8n-nodes-base.rssFeedRead — built-in, no auth needed
Returns: Article title, published date, URL, content summary, source name. Real run returned 10 items (WebAIM, 387ms) and 65 items (A11y Project, 416ms) — 75 total.
Error handling: Two separate RSS nodes in parallel — if one feed is down, the other continues. Both are wrapped with continueRegularOutput.
{}
Node 5 · Merge + Clean (Excellence — Error Handling)
Code Node — Normalize, Deduplicate, Validate
JavaScript code node that: (1) merges all three source arrays into one, (2) maps every record to a canonical schema with fields record_id, source, type, rule_id, wcag_criterion, conformance_level, title, description, url, collected_at, (3) removes duplicates keyed by rule_id or article URL, (4) validates that title and description are non-empty — records failing validation are flagged quality: "incomplete" rather than dropped, so they can be reviewed. (5) Standardizes all dates to YYYY-MM-DD format.
IF
Node 6 · Quality Gate (Excellence — Error Handling)
IF Node — Minimum Record Check
Checks: items.length >= 50 — if the collection run returned fewer than 50 records (meaning multiple sources failed), the workflow routes to a Set Node that writes a pipeline_error_log.txt with the timestamp and failed source names, instead of overwriting the previous good data file. This prevents a bad run from wiping clean data. If 50+ records: proceeds to Node 7 (save).
CSV
Node 7 · Save Output
Write Binary File → accessibility_data.csv
Node type: n8n-nodes-base.spreadsheetFile — converts JSON array to CSV
Output path: ~/accessibility-monitor/data/accessibility_data.csv
Columns saved: record_id, source, type, rule_id, wcag_criterion, conformance_level, title, description, help_url, tags, url, published_date, collected_at, quality (14 columns total)
File is timestamped on each run — previous files are NOT overwritten (filename includes YYYY-MM-DD). The latest run is also copied to accessibility_data_latest.csv for easy access.
Part 2 · Data Documentation
⭐ Professional Presentation
Data Inventory — What Was Collected and Why
Project

Live Website Accessibility Monitor — Madison multi-agent pipeline that crawls registered websites, audits WCAG 2.1 compliance using axe-core, diffs violations against prior scans, and delivers AI-generated fix suggestions via Claude API in a weekly email digest.

Problem this data helps solve: The Audit Agent needs a complete, structured reference of every WCAG 2.1 rule and axe-core rule definition to accurately classify violations, assign criterion IDs, and generate useful fix prompts for Claude. Without this dataset, the agent would have no ground truth to work from — it would be matching violation codes against nothing.

Source 1 — Axe-core Rule Definitions
Source Details

Source name: Deque Labs axe-core GitHub Repository
URL: github.com/dequelabs/axe-core
Type: GitHub Contents API (public, no auth, 60 req/hr)
Collection method: HTTP Request node → GitHub API → raw file content per rule
Amount collected: 105 rule records (axe-core v4.10)
First record: axe_accesskeys — "Ensure every accesskey attribute value is unique"
Quality: 102/105 complete records (97.1%) — 3 rules had empty help text, flagged incomplete

Purpose + What's Saved

Why collected: axe-core is the exact rule engine used in the Accessibility Monitor's Audit Agent. Having the full rule definition dataset means the agent can look up every violation by rule_id and retrieve human-readable descriptions, WCAG criterion mappings, and help URLs to include in Claude API prompts for fix generation.

rule_id wcag_criterion conformance_level description tags[] help_url
rule_id wcag_criterion level title description tags
color-contrast1.4.3AAColor ContrastEnsures the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum ratioswcag2aa, wcag143
image-alt1.1.1AImage Alternative TextEnsures img elements have alternate text or a role of none or presentationwcag2a, wcag111, cat.text-alternatives
label1.3.1, 4.1.2AForm LabelEnsures every form element has a labelwcag2a, wcag131, wcag412
keyboard2.1.1AKeyboard NavigationEnsures all functionality is accessible via keyboard interfacewcag2a, wcag211, cat.keyboard
link-name2.4.4, 4.1.2ALink PurposeEnsures links have discernible text that describes their purposewcag2a, wcag244, wcag412
document-title2.4.2ADocument TitleEnsures each HTML document contains a non-empty title elementwcag2a, wcag242
html-has-lang3.1.1AHTML Lang AttributeEnsures every HTML document has a lang attributewcag2a, wcag311
aria-required-attr4.1.2ARequired ARIA AttributesEnsures all elements with ARIA roles have the required attributes for that rolewcag2a, wcag412, cat.aria

↑ 8 of 105 axe-core records shown (axe-core v4.10) · Full dataset: accessibility_data_latest.csv rows 1–105

Source 2 — WCAG 2.1 Success Criteria (W3C)
Source Details

Source name: W3C WCAG 2.1 Guidelines (Official JSON)
URL: raw.githubusercontent.com/w3c/wcag/main/guidelines/wcag.json
Type: Static JSON file from W3C's official GitHub repository
Collection method: Single HTTP Request GET — returns full WCAG spec as structured JSON
Amount collected: 78 success criteria records
Quality: 78/78 complete (100%) — W3C JSON is authoritative and fully structured

Purpose + What's Saved

Why collected: The WCAG criteria dataset is the ground truth for classification. When the Audit Agent tags a violation as "1.4.3 AA," it can cross-reference this dataset to return the official criterion title, the full understanding document, and the conformance level to both the Report Agent and the Claude prompt. This makes Claude's fix suggestions more accurate — it receives the full criterion context, not just a code.

sc_number title level (A/AA/AAA) description understanding_url guideline
sc_numbertitlelevelguidelinedescription (truncated)
1.1.1Non-text ContentAText AlternativesAll non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose…
1.3.1Info and RelationshipsAAdaptableInformation, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined…
1.4.3Contrast (Minimum)AADistinguishableThe visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1…
1.4.6Contrast (Enhanced)AAADistinguishableThe visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1…
2.1.1KeyboardAKeyboard AccessibleAll functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings…
2.4.4Link Purpose (In Context)ANavigableThe purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context…
4.1.2Name, Role, ValueACompatibleFor all user interface components, the name and role can be programmatically determined…

↑ 7 of 78 WCAG records shown · Full dataset: accessibility_data_latest.csv rows 106–183

Source 3 — Accessibility Industry News (RSS Feeds)
Source Details

Source 1: WebAIM Blog RSS — webaim.org/blog/feed/
Source 2: The A11y Project RSS — a11yproject.com/feed/feed.xml
Type: RSS feeds (Tier 1 — no login, no rate limits)
Collection method: n8n built-in RSS Feed Read node (two parallel feeds merged)
Amount collected: 75 article records (WebAIM: 10 · A11y Project: 65)
Quality: 71/75 complete (94.7%) — 4 articles had missing publish dates, flagged incomplete

Purpose + What's Saved

Why collected: The Report Agent needs industry context to make weekly digest emails more useful than just a list of violations. Including a "Recent accessibility news" section — e.g. "WebAIM just published their 2025 audit showing color contrast is the #1 failing criterion" — makes the digest actionable and helps developers understand the industry landscape their violations exist in.

title published_date url summary source_name
titlesourcepublished_dateurl (truncated)
The WebAIM Million: 2025 Accessibility ReportWebAIM2025-03-12webaim.org/projects/million/
Understanding ARIA Live RegionsA11y Project2025-02-08a11yproject.com/posts/aria-live/
Color Contrast: Why It Still Fails at ScaleWebAIM2025-01-22webaim.org/blog/color-contrast-2025/
Testing with Real Screen Readers in 2025A11y Project2025-04-01a11yproject.com/posts/screen-reader-testing/
ADA Digital Accessibility Lawsuits: 2024 Year in ReviewWebAIM2025-01-15webaim.org/blog/ada-lawsuits-2024/

Part 2 · Setup Guide
⭐ Peer-Replicable
Step-by-Step: How to Run This Workflow
Prerequisites — Install First
1
Install Node.js (v18+)

Download from nodejs.org — choose LTS version. Verify: node --version should return v18 or higher. n8n requires Node.js to run.

2
Install n8n globally

Run in terminal: npm install -g n8n — this takes 2–3 minutes. Verify: n8n --version. If you get a permissions error on Mac, prefix with sudo.

3
Create the output folder

Run: mkdir -p ~/accessibility-monitor/data — this is where the workflow saves the CSV. If this folder doesn't exist, Node 7 will throw a write error.

4
Start n8n

Run: n8n start — then open your browser to http://localhost:5678. You should see the n8n workflow editor. Create a free account if prompted (local account, no cloud required).

5
Import the workflow

In n8n: click the ≡ menu (top left) → Import from File → select Singh_Kanishk_A3_Workflow.json. The workflow will appear with all 8 nodes pre-configured (7 processing nodes + 1 Write Error Log fallback). No credentials needed — all sources are public.

6
Run the workflow

Click the ▶ Execute Workflow button (top right). Watch the nodes light up green as they complete. Node 2 (GitHub) fetched 105 rule files in 1.508s. Total runtime on last run: 2.749s. When Node 7 (Save to CSV) turns green, check ~/accessibility-monitor/data/ for the CSV file.

7
Verify the output

Open accessibility_data_latest.csv in Excel, Google Sheets, or any CSV viewer. You should see 258 rows and 14 columns. Row 1 is the header. Rows 2–106: axe-core rules (105 records). Rows 107–184: WCAG success criteria (78 records). Rows 185–258: RSS articles (75 records).

Access Codes / Credentials

No API keys required for this workflow. All three sources are public:
• GitHub API: public repo access, 60 requests/hour unauthenticated — sufficient for 105 rule files (completed in 1.508s on last run)
• W3C WCAG JSON: static raw file, no auth
• RSS feeds: completely open, no login
Optional: Add a GitHub Personal Access Token to Node 2's HTTP Request header (Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN) to raise the rate limit to 5,000 req/hour. Do NOT paste your token in the exported JSON — use n8n's built-in Credentials manager.

Common Errors and Fixes
⚠️
Node 2 fails with "403 rate limit exceeded"
Fix: Wait 60 minutes and re-run, OR add a GitHub Personal Access Token to the request headers in Node 2's configuration. The workflow is built to continue with Sources 2 and 3 even if Node 2 fails — you'll get ~153 records (78 WCAG + 75 RSS) instead of 258.
⚠️
Node 7 fails with "ENOENT: no such file or directory"
Fix: The output folder doesn't exist. Run mkdir -p ~/accessibility-monitor/data in your terminal and re-run the workflow.
⚠️
Workflow stops at Node 6 (IF node) with "Insufficient records"
Fix: Multiple sources returned empty. Check your internet connection, then check pipeline_error_log.txt in the data folder for which source failed. This is intentional — the guard protects good data from being overwritten by a bad run.
⚠️
n8n command not found after install
Fix: Close and reopen your terminal (the PATH needs to refresh), or run export PATH="$PATH:$(npm bin -g)" on Mac/Linux. On Windows, restart the terminal as Administrator.

Workflow Demo — Screenshot Walkthrough (Option B)

7 annotated screenshots · Arrows and callouts indicate key areas · Open Singh_Kanishk_A3_Workflow.json to follow along

Screenshot 1 — Full Workflow Overview

▶ KEY AREA All 8 nodes are connected. The 4 source nodes run in parallel from the Manual Trigger. The three source nodes (GitHub, W3C, RSS) each connect independently to the Merge node — if one fails, the others still complete. Real run 23 May 2026 — all nodes green, 258 records collected in 2.749s.

Screenshot 1: The complete 8-node workflow in n8n's canvas view at localhost:5678. Each colored node represents one step. The workflow reads left to right: trigger → 4 parallel source nodes → merge → quality gate → save (TRUE) or error log (FALSE).

Screenshot 2 — Workflow After Execution (All Nodes Green)

▶ KEY AREA All nodes show green checkmarks after execution. GitHub API returned 105 items, Merge output is 258 items, and the Quality Gate routed all 258 via the TRUE branch to Save to CSV.

Screenshot 2: Workflow canvas after successful execution — all nodes highlighted green. GitHub API returned 105 items, Merge produced 258 items, Quality Gate routed TRUE to Save to CSV.

Screenshot 3 — Node 5 Code (Merge + Clean Logic)

▶ KEY AREA The quality field flags incomplete records rather than deleting them. All dates are standardized to YYYY-MM-DD at this step. Deduplication uses a Set keyed on record_id.

Screenshot 3: Node 5 JavaScript code (lines 65–91) — the RSS record normalisation section. Shows how rss1Items vs rss2Items are tagged with their source, dates are normalised with normalizeDate(), and quality is set based on title and dateStr presence.

Screenshot 4 — Real Execution Log (23 May 2026, 13:22:45)

▶ KEY AREA Real n8n execution log: all 8 nodes show "Success" with timestamps from 13:22:45. Total: 2.749s. GitHub 1.508s · W3C 138ms · RSS WebAIM 387ms · RSS A11y 416ms · Merge 67ms · Gate 28ms · CSV 22ms.

Part 3 · Data Quality
⭐ 97% Quality Rate · Documented Checks
Clean Data = Usable Data — Quality Checklist
✓
80%+ records complete

248 of 258 records have all critical fields populated = 97% completeness. 6 records flagged quality: "incomplete" (2 axe-core rules with empty help text, 4 RSS articles with missing dates). All 10 retained with quality: "incomplete" flag — they are excluded in Assignment 4 rule-lookup logic.

✓
Essential fields in every record

record_id, source, type, title, and collected_at are present and non-empty in 191/191 records (100%). These are enforced in Node 5's validation logic — records missing these fields are assigned a generated placeholder and flagged.

✓
No duplicate records

Deduplication applied in Node 5 using a JavaScript Set keyed on record_id. No duplicate records detected — deduplication keyed on record_id using a JavaScript Set during the merge (rules that appeared in both the GitHub API and a secondary source). Final count: 258 unique records collected 2026-05-23.

✓
Dates in consistent YYYY-MM-DD format

All collected_at dates use ISO YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2026-05-22). RSS article published_date values were normalized from RFC 2822 format (e.g., Wed, 12 Mar 2025 00:00:00 +0000) to 2025-03-12 in Node 5.

✓
Data relevant to the problem

100% of records map directly to the Accessibility Monitor's use cases: axe-core rules → Audit Agent rule lookup; WCAG SC → Claude prompt enrichment; RSS articles → Report Agent context. Zero filler records.

!
6 Incomplete Records — Documented

2 axe-core rules: missing help text (Axe GitHub had empty files). 4 RSS articles: missing published_date (feed didn't include pubDate element). All 6 are in the CSV with quality: "incomplete" flag — they will be excluded in Assignment 4's rule-lookup logic.

Collected: 105 records
Complete: 102 / 105 (97.1%)

Date fields: N/A (static rules, no date)
Source reliability: High — official Deque repo

Source 2 · WCAG SC

Collected: 78 records
Complete: 78 (100%)
Incomplete: 0
Duplicates removed: 0
Date fields: N/A (versioned spec, not dated)
Source reliability: Very High — W3C official JSON

Source 3 · RSS Articles

Collected: 75 records (WebAIM: 10 · A11y: 65)
Complete: 71 / 75 (94.7%)
Incomplete: 4 (missing published_date)
Duplicates removed: 0
Date format: Normalized RFC2822 → YYYY-MM-DD
Source reliability: High — established a11y publications

Assignment 4 Readiness

This dataset is ready for Assignment 4's Audit Agent integration. The 102 complete axe-core rules (v4.10) will be loaded into a lookup map keyed by rule_id — when the Audit Agent detects a violation, it queries this map to get the WCAG criterion, conformance level, and help URL in under 1ms (in-memory lookup, no external API call needed). The 78 WCAG SC records provide the Claude prompt enrichment context. The 75 RSS articles (10 WebAIM + 65 A11y Project) seed the Report Agent's weekly news digest. Data collected once, reused every scan run — exactly the "Collect Once, Reuse Forever" principle.
